concerns.analysis: The source PDF contains meaningful organizational logos on the first page (the NCDENR logo at the top and the 'One NorthCarolina Naturally' logo at the bottom right). In the rendered HTML output, these images are missing and have been replaced by plain text placeholders lacking proper `` tags.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Page 1 contains the NCDENR agency logo at the top and the 'One NorthCarolina Naturally' logo at the bottom right.
concerns.output_quote:
The HTML contains literal text placeholders `

[Image: Logo of the North Carolina Department of Environment and Natural Resources (NCDENR)]

` and `

[Image: One NorthCarolina Naturally logo]

` but completely lacks the required `` elements.

concerns.description: Displaced hyperlinks: 4 link(s) extracted as standalone elements instead of inline text
concerns.analysis: standalone_links=4, examples=['http://portal.ncdenr.org/web/wq/swp/ws/401/certsandpermits/m', 'mailto:katie.merritt@ncdenr.gov', 'http://portal.ncdenr.org/web/wq/swp/ws/401/certsandpermits/s']. Links should be inline within paragraphs, not separate blocks.

concerns.description: Header/footer elements retained in output (8): Wetlands, Buffers, Stormwater - Compliance and Permitting Un; An Equal Opportunity \ Affirmative Action Employer; Page 2 of 7
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=8, pages_affected=7/7. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML — the renderer failed to strip it. This is a structural accessibility issue.

concerns.description: List broken by page break (1 instance(s)): p4→p5: list item ends “…talled in all streams as outlined in the”, continues as paragraph
concerns.analysis: split_list_count=1. Lists that span page breaks should be extracted as a single continuous structure. Split lists affect reading order and navigability for assistive technology.

concerns.description: Lists converted incorrectly: 1 ordered list(s) have alphabetical markers (A, B, C) rendered as numeric — e.g. "a. All work must be conducted “in the dry” condition will be"
concerns.analysis: alpha_numeric_confusion=1. Ordered list items start with sequential letter prefixes (A., B., C.) but are rendered as numeric lists, losing the alphabetical semantics.

concerns.description: Table header markup issues: 1 table(s) have a data row marked as header: "Corps’ Regional General Permit"
concerns.analysis: data_in_th=0, datarow_as_header=1, no_th_tables=0. Tables use <th> for data values, have a data row mismarked as a header row, or lack <th> elements entirely. Screen readers rely on <th> to announce column/row context.

concerns.description: Footnote(s) converted to bulleted list: 1 unordered list(s) after table(s) appear to be footnotes
concerns.analysis: footnote_as_list_count=1. Examples: 1 item(s): "Please note that the US Army Corps of Engineers is". Table footnotes (often marked with *, †, ‡) were converted to <ul><li> elements instead of paragraphs. This changes the semantic meaning — screen readers announce them as list items rather than explanatory notes.
